title: failure when running `sudo curl -v -s --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock http://d/v1.0.0/libpod/info`
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I'm new to podman, but I'm trying to get the nomad driver to work with podman

I'm having the same problem as described here (https://discuss.hashicorp.com/t/nomad-podman-rhel8-driver-difficulties/21877/2)

The comments there suggest that in order for the nomad driver to work the following should succeed: 

> The following should work as a way to test that that the driver can reach out to the podman api sudo curl -v -s --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock http://d/v1.0.0/libpod/info

So I've tried running this on my RHEL 8, with podman version 3.0.2 and I get the following: 

```
$ sudo curl -v -s --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock http://d/v1.0.0/libpod/info
*   Trying /run/podman/podman.sock...
* Immediate connect fail for /run/podman/podman.sock: No such file or directory
* Closing connection 0
```

So before getting nomad to work, it seems like there is a deeper problem. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `sudo curl -v -s --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock http://d/v1.0.0/libpod/info`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Trying /run/podman/podman.sock...
* Immediate connect fail for /run/podman/podman.sock: No such file or directory
* Closing connection 0
 ```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I'm not sure. I expected some kind of success. Downstream, I expected the nomad agent to be able to communicate with podman and start the container specified in the nomad job file. 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
 podman version
Version:      3.0.2-dev
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.13
Built:        Tue Jun  8 03:52:06 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


```
podman-3.0.1-7.module+el8.4.0+11311+9da8acfb.x86_64

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

I have tried, but yum doesn't seem to recognize anything newer than 3.02. 
I've tried yum update and the yum install podman, but it says there is nothing new to install. (If you can advise how to update to 3.1 or 3.2 on RHEL 8, I'd be grateful.

I didn't see my particular issue listed in the troubleshooting guide.

comments.body:
@jeffreycwitt It appears you are not connecting to the running Podman service. You give `--unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock` in your curl command but `podman info` shows 
```
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
```
It would appear you should be using:
`curl -v -s --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock http://d/v1.0.0/libpod/info`

`systemctl --user status podman.socket podman.service` will provide you with the Podman service status.

Thanks so much for your response. I think I may even be having trouble getting the socket open in the first place. 

Here's what I'm getting when I run `$ systemctl --user status podman.socket podman.service`

``` 
$ systemctl --user status podman.socket podman.service
● podman.socket - Podman API Socket
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.socket; disabled; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: inactive (dead)
     Docs: man:podman-system-service(1)
   Listen: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock (Stream)

● podman.service - Podman API Service
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.service; static; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: inactive (dead)
     Docs: man:podman-system-service(1)
```

I tried:
```
$ sudo systemctl enable --now podman.socket
```
Which did the following: 
```
Created symlink /etc/systemd/system/sockets.target.wants/podman.socket → /usr/lib/systemd/system/podman.socket.
```
but the status request is giving me the same response. 
```
[jcwitt@facresearch09-ad-loyola-edu ~]$ systemctl --user status podman.socket podman.service
● podman.socket - Podman API Socket
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.socket; disabled; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: inactive (dead)
     Docs: man:podman-system-service(1)
   Listen: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock (Stream)

● podman.service - Podman API Service
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.service; static; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: inactive (dead)
     Docs: man:podman-system-service(1)
[jcwitt@facresearch09-ad-loyola-edu ~]$ curl -v -s --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock http://d/v1.0.0/libpod/info
*   Trying /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock...
* Immediate connect fail for /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock: No such file or directory
* Closing connection 0

```

I'm definitely a newbie (as you can tell) but I appreciate any advice you can give.

@jeffreycwitt From contrib/systemd/README.md, there is a section for `user (podman service run as given user aka "rootless")` did you follow those instructions to copy the necessary unit files? I ask because your status above points to `/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.socket`. Or maybe a `systemctl --user daemon-reload` is needed?

Ok, I definitely did NOT do that. Thanks so much for the pointer. I just copied the example files to run the socket and service as root. 

And voilà, success, which also seemed to  lead to in running nomad with the podman driver. Huzzah!

```
 sudo nomad run webapp.nomad
==> Monitoring evaluation "731d9964"
    Evaluation triggered by job "demo-webapp"
    Evaluation within deployment: "60823130"
    Evaluation status changed: "pending" -> "complete"
==> Evaluation "731d9964" finished with status "complete"
$ ==> Monitoring evaluation "75acf504"
```

Thanks so much for your help. I think we can definitely close this issue.
